steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The notes show two key facts: syllable rate (SR) varied widely across the 17 languages, but information rate (IR) was roughly the same (~39 bits/sec) for all of them. The correct choice must capture this contrast between varying SR and steady IR.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — syllable rates differed but information rates were roughly equal: Captures the core contrast from the notes. ✓
  • Choice B — focuses only on syllable rate differences: Ignores the finding about information rate, which is the more surprising result. ✗
  • Choice C — claims information rate also varied: Contradicts the notes, which say IR was roughly constant. ✗
  • Choice D — adds details not supported by the notes: Introduces information beyond what the notes contain. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
